intro: Diasperus glabrescens, also known as Zanthoxylum nitidum, is a plant species native to parts of Asia, classified as a member of the Rutaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus glabrescens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Diasperus glabrescens is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and topical creams.
